sourceName: Electrical, Mechanical and Hardware Engineering CV
resumeLength: One page for internship/job resumes; longer CV only for research-heavy applications.

formattingRules.fontRecommendation: Readable serif or sans-serif; compact technical hierarchy with aligned dates.
formattingRules.bulletStyle: 1-2 line bullets, technical noun first when helpful.
formattingRules.dateFormat: Consistent month/year, right aligned where possible.

writingRules.bulletFormula: Action + engineered object/system + method/tool + constraint/test condition + validated output or performance result
quantificationRules: Prefer concrete scope: sample size, stakeholder count, case volume, event size, model accuracy, project duration, budget, risk, or response time when supported by source facts.
When exact numbers are absent, use bounded evidence such as tool, method, deliverable, audience, or process scope; never invent metrics.
Use metrics differently by domain: technical performance for engineering, compliance/accuracy for audit, writing/research output for law and policy, service reliability for hospitality.

exampleBullets.strong: Designed and tested a PLC-based automation module, documenting sensor logic and failure cases for repeatable lab validation.
exampleBullets.reason: Names the system, method, and validation output instead of vague participation.

exampleBullets.strong: Modeled control-system response in MATLAB/Simulink and compared parameter sensitivity across three operating scenarios.
exampleBullets.reason: Turns a tool list into a method-driven engineering contribution.

scoringCriteria.description: Conventional headings, readable layout, aligned dates, consistent spacing, and parser-friendly text.
Prominent sections and bullets match the target role and market context.
Bullets show action, method, scope, and outcome without unsupported claims.
Specific, concise, role-native language with no inflated AI phrasing.
Structure and density match the selected role, country, and CV/resume style.

promptSnippets.title: Electrical, Mechanical and Hardware Engineering CV routing rule
promptSnippets.content: When the target role matches Electrical / Mechanical / Hardware Engineering, use Electrical, Mechanical and Hardware Engineering CV as the primary industry route and adopt this formula: Action + engineered object/system + method/tool + constraint/test condition + validated output or performance result.

promptSnippets.title: Electrical, Mechanical and Hardware Engineering CV language guardrail
promptSnippets.content: Preserve domain-native evidence for Electrical / Mechanical / Hardware Engineering; do not convert the profile into a generic tech, finance, or consulting resume unless the user target explicitly requires it.

sourceReferences.label: Local 123456 CV sample corpus
sourceReferences.note: 56 newly added CV/resume samples from docs/sample-resume-knowledge; abstracted into rules only, without copying resume text.

sourceReferences.label: Existing local resume-learning corpus
